rationale: The citing paper references Lowe and Haws (2017) mainly as part of grouped prior-work citations showing that pitch has been studied in auditory/sensory marketing. It is used to support broad background claims (e.g., that pitch can influence brand or product perceptions), not as a specific theory, mechanism, method, or empirical benchmark that the new studies directly build on. The core conceptual and empirical development in the citing paper centers on timbral roughness/smoothness rather than extending or refining the cited paper's pitch-size framework.
